content: Health sector climate responses include: (1) Adaptation - heat early warning systems (reducing heat mortality 20-30%), vector surveillance and control, climate-resilient infrastructure (flood-proof hospitals), drought-resistant food systems; (2) Mitigation - healthcare decarbonization (healthcare contributes 4-5% of global emissions, anesthesia gases and inhalers are potent greenhouse gases), sustainable supply chains, plant-based hospital meals; (3) Co-benefits - active transport (cycling/walking) reducing both emissions and cardiovascular disease, reduced air pollution from fossil fuel transition preventing 4-8 million premature deaths annually. Health adaptation funding remains inadequate: <5% of climate finance targets health. Successful examples: France's heat wave plan reduced excess mortality from 15,000 (2003) to 2,000 (2006 heat wave of similar intensity).
